MILITARY GOVERNMENT O F SANTO DOMINGO.
Executive Order No. 763.
AUTHORIZING BARAHONA WATER CONTRACT, AND
FOR OTHER PURPOSES.
0 . G. No. 3336.
WHEREAS, by Governmental Resolution, dated August 7,
1920, T h e Barahona Company, Inc., was authorized t o take up
t o 142 litres of water per second from the “Palomino” brook in
t h e Barahona Province, for domestic and industrial purposes,
and to construct a dam, canals, pipe lines, a hydraulic plant and
other indispensable works for the distribution and use of said
water ;
WHEREAS, The Barahona Cunipany, Inc., has already
constructed t h e dam. pipe lines, canals, water supply plant, and
other works appurtenant theretu, an,! is a t present conveying
water from the “Paloxiiio’’ l~r*:,okt u it point situated in the
neighborhood of the zity of Barahona, ail3 also near the Govern-
ment’s public pier in the port of Barahona ;

WHEREAS, a water supply f o r vessels and steamers visit-

WHEREAS, The Barahona Company, Inc., is willling t o

NOW T H E R E F O R E , By virtue of the powers vesited in the
Military Government of Santo Domingo, the folslowing Executive
Order is hereby promulgated :
ARTICLE 1. The Barahona Company, Inc., is hereby
authorized to sell to the Ayirntanzieiito of the Commune of E a w -

from the “Palomino” brook, t o be used for domestic and indiis-
trial purposes, in accordance with such prices, terms, and other

ARTICLE 3. E’or the purposes nwntioiied i n the foregoing

ARTICLE 4. I t is hereby declared t h a t the furnishing and
sale of water, a s provided in this Executive Order, does not nor
will not prejudice the rights acguired by The Barahona Company,
Inc., through t h e Concession of August 7, 1920, authorizing i t t o
t a k e water f r o m the “Palomino” brook, or the rights acquired
by said Company t o supply \$ater t o persons and enterprises to
which it furnishes such liquid in accordance with Governmental
Resollution of February 25, 1921, published in Official Gazette
No. 3201, which Concession and Resolution a r e hereby ratified
and continue in effect in all their parts.
ARTICLE 5 . All laws o r parts of laws in conflict herewith
a r e hereby repealed.
HARRY L E E ,
